outcomes: Listen attentively to extended stories, instructions, and conversations for details
Respond accurately and appropriately to complex verbal prompts and questions
Communicate ideas, desires, feelings, and personal experiences clearly and fluently using complete sentences
Take turns constructively during structured classroom conversations and peer discussions
Identify, blend, and segment rhyming words, syllables, and initial sounds in spoken words
Produce letter sounds accurately for basic consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) patterns
Track text visually from left to right and top to bottom with emerging consistency
Recognize sight words and common functional words within the print environment
Predict outcomes or next steps in a story based on illustrations and contextual clues
Recall and sequence main events or characters from a read-aloud narrative
Form upper and lower case letters legibly using correct directional strokes
Write personal names and simple three-letter words independently with proper spacing
Sort and classify a collection of concrete objects based on two simultaneous attributes such as size and color
Create and extend simple repeating patterns using concrete objects, shapes, or drawings
Count objects sequentially up to 20 using concrete representations and one-to-one correspondence
Represent quantities up to 10 using concrete objects, drawings, and numerals
Demonstrate the concept of addition as putting together objects up to a sum of 9
Demonstrate the concept of subtraction as taking away objects from a group up to 9
Compare and order objects based on mass (heavy/light) and capacity (holds more/holds less)
Identify days of the week and describe daily routines using time-relative terms like morning, afternoon, and night
Identify, name, and draw basic two-dimensional shapes including circles, triangles, rectangles, and squares
Recognize and point out basic shapes within objects in the immediate environment
Observe, describe, and record daily weather conditions using symbols (sunny, rainy, windy, cloudy)
Identify appropriate clothing, shelter, and activities for different weather conditions
Identify and care for common plants and domestic animals within the locality
State basic parts of a plant and recognize safe versus dangerous plants and animals
Identify and roleplay different community helpers (teachers, doctors, police officers, farmers) and their contributions
Demonstrate respect for diverse cultures, norms, and roles within the local neighborhood
Practice proper disposal of waste and conservation of water and electricity at home and school
Demonstrate care and responsibility for personal and shared school property
Execute complex physical skills including catching, throwing, kicking, and bouncing a ball with control
Maintain body dynamic balance while navigating obstacles or participating in structured relays
Create functional and decorative artifacts using techniques like paper folding, weaving, painting, and clay modeling
Express personal imagination and stories through distinct drawings and artwork
Perform short songs, singing games, and simple rhythmic dances individually and in groups
Dramatize simple situational roles, stories, or emotional states during creative play

sub_strands.sub_strand: Early Life and Family of Jesus
outcomes: Narrate stories about the birth, childhood, and family background of Jesus Christ
Relate the obedience and growth of young Jesus to their own daily choices and family relationships
Demonstrate key Christian values such as sharing, honesty, kindness, and love during peer interactions
Apply simple biblical moral lessons to resolve everyday conflicts peacefully
